exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, explicit focus on the prescribed title throughout
Effective integration of two areas of knowledge with wellâanalysed real-world examples
Clear, coherent structure with logically developed arguments
Evaluation of counter-perspectives and consideration of implications of knowledge claims
weaknesses: Occasional descriptive passages that lack deeper evaluative insight
Under-use of TOK terminology and aspects of the knowledge framework
Redundancies and verbosity in places (especially in the conclusion)
Inconsistent citation style and a thesis that could be more precisely stated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment indicates that the essay meets the criteria for the 9â10 band, with a sustained focus on the title, clear and coherent argumentation supported by pertinent examples, awareness of counter-claims, and consideration of implications. Minor issuesâsuch as occasional descriptive sections, under-explored TOK terminology, and verbosityâlimit the work from achieving the full sophistication of a perfect score, justifying a final mark of 9 out of 10.

annotations.comment: This analysis correctly identifies biases but could evaluate how social desirability and self-selection affect certainty as a way of knowing.
This paragraph effectively synthesizes the online survey discussion but restates previous points; consider exploring a counterargument to advance the analysis.
Strength: the student effectively challenges the absoluteness of âalways,â demonstrating nuanced evaluation of the title.
Strength: clear explanation of how STEM enables atomic-level observations, enhancing knowledge in materials science.
The concluding paragraph should explicitly restate the thesis and directly address the key term âalways.â
Good synthesis across both AoKs; enhance by reflecting on the implications for knowledge justification in each area.
The concluding sentence is comprehensive but verbose; suggest tightening and explicitly linking back to the knowledge question wording.
This conclusion effectively links back to the prescribed title, reinforcing that improved tools are not infallible.
Citation formatting is inconsistent and incomplete; adopt a standard academic referencing style for clarity.
This paragraph synthesizes natural sciences examples effectively but could be more concise to avoid redundancy.
Good contextualization of online surveys; integrate a knowledge question to maintain explicit TOK focus throughout this section.
At this point, the student hasn't provided a clear thesis or roadmap for the essay. Suggest including a concise thesis statement outlining the two AoKs and the stance.
The thesis statement here is generic; refine it to explicitly state the studentâs position on whether improved tools always yield better knowledge.
To deepen the analysis, the student should discuss which ways of knowing (e.g., emotion, reason) influence interpretation of polygraph results.
The student effectively restates the prescribed title, demonstrating clear engagement with the prompt.
The student situates the role of tools historically, giving relevant examples, but could tighten analysis by directly linking artificial intelligence to the knowledge question.
The polygraph case is introduced effectively, but the student should connect it explicitly to the knowledge question using TOK terminology.
The Juan Rivera case is compelling; ensure the source is credible (e.g., academic article) rather than solely Wikipedia to strengthen the argument.
The sentence is overly long and repetitive; suggest splitting it to improve clarity and coherence.
The Pew study example is specific, but lacks critical evaluation of the sampling methodâs impact on knowledge validity.
The bacteriophage example is well-chosen; strengthen it by citing primary scientific literature rather than Wikipedia.
To deepen the analysis, discuss limitations of electron microscopy, such as sample preparation artifacts affecting knowledge claims.
Consider incorporating TOK concepts such as âbiasâ or âreliabilityâ to deepen the analysis of the polygraphâs limitations.
The informal reference to âthe computer writing this ToK essayâ disrupts the academic tone; remove or formalize this phrase.
Suggest referencing aspects of the knowledge framework (e.g., scope, methodology) to explain why tool efficacy varies.
Evaluate the implications of the seeding experiments on the reliability and scope of meteorological knowledge.
Many in-text references do not clearly match works cited entries; ensure each citation is traceable and correctly formatted.
Strong introduction to the electron microscope; tie it explicitly to the knowledge question by discussing certainty or scale of knowledge.
The inline citations are inconsistent; adopt a uniform academic referencing style within the essay body.
Introduce a specific knowledge question for the natural sciences section to maintain TOK structure and clarity.
This sentence ends abruptly; ensure each sentence presents a complete thought for coherence.
The paragraph begins with âwould mirrorâ lacking a clear subject; revise to improve paragraph cohesion.
Strong evaluation of unpredictability in Project Stormfury; consider connecting this to the knowledge frameworkâs methodology element.
Strength: clear conclusion that tool effectiveness depends on system alignment, reinforcing a nuanced perspective.
The works cited list relies heavily on Wikipedia sources; incorporate more peer-reviewed or primary references to strengthen credibility.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
